page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth, with large block read-only speeds reaching 211.52 GB/sec and write speeds at 99.63 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core processing is also robust, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 16013.0. The CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for deep learning and matrix operations, which support solid text and image processing performance.

From a resource density perspective, the n4-standard-32 offers a balanced hardware profile that avoids the premium costs associated with specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. Additionally, while the server handles small to medium language model inference efficiently, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation, making it less suitable for heavy generative AI workloads without external accelerators.

This server is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and text or image processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 128 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel processor, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, the instance delivers strong performance in memory bandwidth, with large block reads at 211.52 GB/sec, and multi-core processing, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 16013.0. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-effective profile for standard compute needs, though it presents a tradeoff in storage latency due to the absence of local disks. It is highly suitable for static web serving, software compilation, text and image processing, and medium-scale database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, designed for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, strong memory bandwidth, and advanced matrix extensions for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM (4 GB per core)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Strong memory bandwidth with 211.52 GB/sec large block reads
No local storage or GPU, relying on network-attached storage
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and text processing
